No Poverty.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Economic Inequality' (Week 13) and 'the Decline of the Welfare State in Less-Developed Countries' (Week 10 reading), directly addressing policy frameworks for poverty reduction and social protection (Targets 1.3, 1.b).
evidence: the Decline of the Welfare State in Less-Developed Countries

Zero Hunger.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to hunger, food security, agricultural productivity, or sustainable food systems.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: A reading in Week 12, 'International Relations After COVID-19,' provides a minimal, inferred link to global health risks and management (Target 3.d), though it's not a central theme.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) focused on developing knowledge systems, analytical frameworks, and original research skills (Target 4.4), and fostering international perspectives and civic values (Target 4.7).
evidence: 具備國際觀點與公民價值知能
協助學生培養感興趣的研究主題，進而設計並撰寫原創性的研究論文

Gender Equality.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any explicit or inferable content related to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to affordable and clean energy services or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course is fundamentally about 'Political Economy,' with explicit weeks on 'International Trade,' 'International Finance,' and 'Development and FDI,' directly addressing economic growth, productivity, and financial services (Targets 8.1, 8.2, 8.10).
evidence: Foreign Direct Investment Inflows to Developing Countries

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly dedicates weeks to 'The Political Economy of Technological Development' and 'US-China Tech Competition,' directly addressing enhancing scientific research, technological capabilities, and innovation (Targets 9.5, 9.b).
evidence: 科技發展的政治經濟學 (The Political Economy of Technological Development)
美中科技競爭與貿易戰 (US-China Tech Competition and Trade War)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Economic Inequality' (Week 13) and 'Political Economy of Migration' (Week 9), directly addressing income inequality (Target 10.1, 10.4) and migration policies (Target 10.7).
evidence: 移民的政治經濟學 (Political Economy of Migration)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to sustainable cities, housing, transport, or urban planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Week 11's topic 'Environment and Resource Curse' and readings on 'International Political Economy and the Environment' and 'greening global supply chains' directly relate to sustainable resource management and production practices (Targets 12.2, 12.6).
evidence: International Political Economy and the Environment

Climate Action.reason: The 'Environment and Resource Curse' topic (Week 11) and related readings provide an inferred, albeit weak, link to climate change issues and their integration into policy (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: The course syllabus contains no explicit or inferable content related to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers 'International Governance' (Week 12) and 'Political Economy of National Security' (Week 15), and discusses institutions and geopolitical factors, directly addressing strong institutions and global governance (Targets 16.6, 16.8).
evidence: 國家安全的政治經濟學 (Political Economy of National Security)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's core subject is 'International Political Economy,' with explicit topics on 'International Trade,' 'International Finance,' and 'International Governance,' directly addressing global partnerships, multilateral systems, and policy coherence (Targets 17.10, 17.13, 17.14).
